title: Agent Harness legacy composed-block mapping
description: Schema for `legacy_composed_mapping.json`. Authored by a consumer when `harness sync` raises EMERGE_LEGACY_UNMAPPED on a composed-class file (i.e. the consumer's file contains non-template, non-block prose that does not match the harness template skeleton). Each entry under `regions` describes ONE such legacy region by exact byte content and tells the sync engine to either map the content into a named local block (`map_to_block`) or discard it (`discard`). Per CS03e / LRN-019. The runtime validator in `lib/composed.mjs` (`validateLegacyMapping`) enforces the same rules at sync time; this schema mirrors them for IDE autocomplete + standalone validation via `harness lint` / `validate-schemas.mjs`. CS03d's `template_prose_hash` evolution detection reduces how often this file is needed but does not eliminate it (cases (b) consumer-edited-prose and (d) no-prior-lock still require it).

$schema.description: Optional self-reference to this schema's $id for IDE autocomplete. Set to 'https://github.com/henrik-me/agent-harness/schemas/legacy-composed-mapping.schema.json' or to a relative path during local development.

_comment.description: Free-form note (single string). Ignored by the sync engine. Useful for recording why each region exists or what the author did to determine the right action.

regions.description: Ordered list of legacy region descriptors. The order does not affect correctness — `validateLegacyMapping` performs multiset accounting against the actual legacy regions detected in the consumer file. Each entry must describe a region by EXACT byte content (whitespace included); a whitespace-only mismatch raises EMERGE_LEGACY_BAD_MAPPING. The runtime enforces `minItems: 1` (an empty `regions` array still leaves all detected legacy content unmapped, so the engine raises EMERGE_LEGACY_UNMAPPED) — the schema mirrors that constraint here so authoring-time validation surfaces it without waiting for sync.

regionEntry.description: A single legacy region descriptor. The `action` field selects between `map_to_block` (preserve content, write it into the named local block) and `discard` (drop content, leave the block empty if no other source). `content` is the EXACT byte string (LF-normalised) from the consumer's file that this entry describes. `additionalProperties` is intentionally NOT set to `false` here — the runtime validator in `lib/composed.mjs validateLegacyMapping` only inspects `action`, `block_id`, and `content`, so extra keys are tolerated for forward-compat (e.g. consumer annotations); the schema mirrors that leniency.

action.description: Action for this region. `map_to_block` → preserve the region's content as the body of the local block named in `block_id` (sync writes provenance: 'migrated-from-legacy' for that block in the lock). `discard` → drop the region; `block_id` MUST be omitted. Any other value (e.g. 'drop', 'keep') is rejected as EMERGE_LEGACY_BAD_MAPPING.

block_id.description: Identifier of the local block to receive this region's content. REQUIRED when `action` is `map_to_block`; FORBIDDEN when `action` is `discard`. Must match the same pattern as composed-block IDs elsewhere in the harness (`^[a-z][a-z0-9.-]*$`) and must be present in the template's local-block markers AND in the consumer's `harness.config.json` `composed.overrides[<file>].local_blocks` allowlist.

content.description: EXACT byte content of the legacy region from the consumer's file (LF-normalised, no leading/trailing whitespace trimming). The runtime validator performs an exact string match against the actual region detected in the file; even a single space difference raises EMERGE_LEGACY_BAD_MAPPING. Use a trailing newline if the region in the file has one.
